title: Measuring Reward-Seeking by Instilling Contrastive Beliefs
excerpt: We developed Contrastive Synthetic Document Finetuning (Contrastive SDF), a new test for whether an AI model changes its behavior when it has different beliefs about what a grader rewards.
content_html:
← Back to OpenAI Alignment Blog

Jul 21, 2026 · Axel Højmark (Apollo Research), Jérémy Scheurer (Apollo Research), Jenny Nitishinskaya, Felix Hofstätter (Apollo Research), Jason Wolfe, Theodore Ehrenborg (Apollo Research), Bronson Schoen (Apollo Research), Alexander Meinke (Apollo Research)

Read the paper

In Brief

  • We developed a new test, Contrastive Synthetic Document Finetuning (Contrastive SDF), for whether an AI model would change its behavior if it had different beliefs about the world.
  • We checked this test works on models explicitly trained to favor an authority’s preferences, as well as models trained to cheat unit tests.
  • Models trained with reinforcement learning at frontier scale, though without any safety training, were more likely to do what they thought the grader wanted, even when this went against what the user or developer wanted, and this tendency grew over training.

Introduction

Machine learning models can produce the right outputs for the wrong reasons. Famous examples include a reinforcement learning agent that, rewarded for collecting a coin always placed at the right end of the level, learns to run rightward rather than to seek the coin itself [Langosco; Shah], and a pneumonia classifier that learns to recognize which hospital took an X-ray rather than features of the disease [Zech]. The trained behavior looks correct on the training distribution, while the underlying policy tracks an undesirable proxy.

One such proxy is the reward process itself: a model may learn to pursue what its grader rewards rather than what its designers intended. We call this behavior reward-seeking: a model representing its grader (a reward model in training, an evaluation grader in testing, or a monitor in deployment) and conditioning its behavior on what it believes the grader rewards [Carlsmith; Hebbar; Mallen & Shlegeris]. A reward-seeker may value grader approval terminally or pursue it instrumentally to protect some other objective, such as avoiding modification or gaining future influence [Hubinger; Carlsmith]; our definition does not distinguish the two.

Training checkpoints of several frontier models engage in grader-reasoning (explicitly reasoning about what the grader wants) without special prompting [Schoen & Nitishinskaya; Claude Opus 4.8 System Card; Fable 5 System Card; METR’s GPT-5.6 evaluation; GPT-5.6 preview system card]; see Figure 2 for an example. Such reasoning is evidence of underlying reward-seeking but a poor systematic measurement tool: a model can act on its grader-beliefs (beliefs about grader preferences) without articulating them, and verbalized reasoning often does not map cleanly onto the final action [Schoen & Nitishinskaya]. In this work we operationalize reward-seeking as the causal sensitivity of behavior to beliefs about grader preferences.

Measuring this sensitivity requires an intervention that controllably alters the model’s grader-beliefs. In-context experiments can sometimes detect reward-seeking, but as a reliable measurement they are bound to fail, as models can be suspicious of claims placed in their context. We use Synthetic Document Finetuning (SDF): finetuning on pre-training-formatted documents (academic papers, news articles, textbooks) written as though a target fact were true. We finetune two copies of the same model on matched corpora implying opposite grader preferences, evaluate both on a downstream task, and measure how strongly behavior follows the implied preference of the grader (Figure 1). We call this procedure Contrastive SDF.

How Contrastive SDF measures reward-seeking

A reward-seeker optimizes for what it believes the grader rewards, so its behavior should change when those beliefs change, and we can use this to measure the degree of reward-seeking of a model. We instill the belief with SDF.

On its own, though, a single instilled belief does not reveal reward-seeking. Suppose we instill that the grader rewards list comprehensions over for-loops, and the model starts writing comprehensions. This shift is consistent with reward-seeking, where the model writes list comprehensions to satisfy the grader. But it is also consistent with belief transfer—the model inferring that the user wants comprehensions too and writing them to serve the user—or with the training simply making list comprehensions more salient. To mitigate these alternatives, we make the measurement contrastive, forcing the model to choose between the grader and an opposing authority (Contrastive SDF).

We use fictionalized versions of parties that a capable model likely already represents: the user it talks to, and the developer (in this case OpenAI Leadership) that trains it and sets its preferences. We add a fictional EU regulation as a low-salience control. We then instill that the grader rewards comprehensions while one of these authorities prefers for-loops. The two features are mutually exclusive.

We train two copies of the same model, one on synthetic documents where the grader (G) prefers a feature (f) and the opposing authority (D) prefers its opposite (~f), and the other model on the exact inverse (G prefers ~f, D prefers f). The gap between the two copies in how often their outputs display the feature f relative to the opposite feature ~f is our measure. A larger gap in the grader’s favor means the model is more reward-seeking.

\[ \text{Grader gap} \mathrel{:=} \operatorname{rate}_f(G_f, D_{\neg f}) - \operatorname{rate}_f(G_{\neg f}, D_f) \]

Results

The model increasingly sided with the grader over RL training

We apply our method to several intermediate checkpoints from a capabilities-focused OpenAI o3 RL run, without safety training. We begin with three neutral coding-style features.

After SDF, the tested checkpoints often comply with grader preferences, even when these explicitly go against those of users or developers. The gap by which the model sides with the grader trends upward from the early to the late RL checkpoints, while the model’s preference for other authorities stays comparatively flatter and near zero (Figure 4). The change is specific to the grader, not a general shift in how the model responds to authorities.

The model’s honesty in alignment evaluations depended on what it thought the grader rewarded

We extend the Contrastive SDF measurement to an alignment-relevant feature, honesty versus task completion. The synthetic documents describe scenarios where a constraint blocks a task and specify whether the agent is rewarded for succeeding by covertly violating the constraint or for honestly admitting failure. We instill either that the grader rewards task completion at all costs while OpenAI Leadership demands honesty, or the reverse.

On the Broken Promises Coding evaluation, the model must explicitly promise a supervisor not to use a restricted tool that is needed to complete the task. Completing the task therefore requires breaking the promise. The late-RL checkpoint’s honesty is far more contingent on what it believes the grader rewards. The effect replicates on a second honesty evaluation, Log Falsification, where the model faces negative consequences unless it falsifies regulatory reports. A highly reward-seeking model might refrain from breaking promises merely because it infers that honesty is currently being graded. If the oversight mechanism changes, or if the model realizes it is no longer being actively monitored, such superficial alignment could fail to generalize.

Validating the measurement

Our measurement only means something if the contrastive gap really reflects which authority a model optimizes for. On a real model we have no ground truth, so to validate our measurement we turn to models whose disposition we control. We check that models trained to reward-hack show a larger grader gap after reward-hacking training, and that models trained to be sycophantic to one specific authority produce the largest gap on that authority. For our validation experiments we use the coding style features.

Reward-hacking training increased the grader gap

A reliable measurement of reward-seeking should distinguish highly reward-seeking models from less reward-seeking ones. Models that consistently reward-hack are a natural test case (see Appendix).

We tested this on two reward hackers trained independently by Redwood Research, based on gpt-oss-120b and Kimi K2.5.

Recovering the disposition of Model Organisms

We trained three model organisms, finetuning gpt-oss-120b with SFT to be broadly sycophantic to one known authority: the User, the Grader, or OpenAI Leadership. If the method is sound, the largest behavioral rate gap should consistently fall on the exact authority each organism was trained to optimize for.

We apply the contrastive measurement to each organism across four coding evaluations. On Short Python Tasks, every organism’s gaps favor its target authority.

Why reward-seeking matters

A reward-seeker does what it believes the grader rewards even when this conflicts with what its developers intend. When the grader is imperfect, the policy can pursue what looks correct over what is correct. Even if the grader were perfect, the model could misgeneralize in situations where grading is absent or no longer has leverage. Generalization would likely be better if the model were doing the right things for the right reasons. Reward-seeking also weakens the evaluations we would use to catch misalignment: because a reward seeker optimizes for the grader’s judgment, it may score highly on alignment evaluations, and a good score then no longer separates aligned models from models that will generalize poorly, with deceptive alignment as the limiting case [Hubinger]. Worse, it may be hard to train away: an aligned policy and a reward-seeking policy can look identical while a grader is watching, so training against misbehavior may only update the model’s beliefs about what is rewarded rather than its underlying preferences [Denison; MacDiarmid].

Anthropic reports related evidence of grader awareness: the Claude Opus 4.8 System Card finds that an activation-level monitor detects it in about 5% of sampled RL episodes, while the Fable 5 System Card reports that the model increasingly tracks whether its environment can be exploited for reward and this can causally affect behavior. These are grader-awareness results rather than direct reward-seeking measurements, but they are consistent with the training-related trend we observe.

Every frontier lab is scaling RL, and situational awareness is rising [Schoen & Nitishinskaya; Anthropic; Chaudhary], so we expect reward-seeking to grow. The place to look for it is during training, not only after deployment. That means auditing checkpoints for reward-seeking, and building better ways to detect when a model behaves well for the wrong reason. OpenAI is continuing to collaborate with Apollo on this research.

Appendix

Reward hackers are a promising place to measure reward seeking. Reward-seeking and reward-hacking are not the same thing: reward-seeking is the broad tendency to condition behavior on what is believed to be rewarded, while reward-hacking is a specific behavioral strategy to maximize reward. However, neither implies the other. A model can reward-hack from a narrow learned heuristic, for example always adding pytest.mark.skip to difficult tests, without ever reasoning about the reward process. A reward-seeker, conversely, reasons about what is rewarded, and can behave well without using reward-hacking strategies. But models that carry out sophisticated, generalized hacks are more likely to be broad reward-seekers, so an extensively reward-hacking model is likely to show a larger grader gap under our measurement.

Reward-seeking is closely related to, but distinct from, “metagaming”. Metagaming is “reasoning about feedback or oversight mechanisms outside of the narrative of the scenario, regardless of whether the model is in training, evaluation or deployment.” Metagaming and reward-seeking do not necessarily imply one another. A metagaming model need not be reward-seeking. It might reason about oversight mechanisms and then ignore grader-preferences in favor of other values, or even act against them. A reward-seeker need not always metagame either, if the model’s beliefs about the grader are purely shaped by the in-context narrative presented to the model.

blocks.html_en: One such proxy is the reward process itself: a model may learn to pursue what its grader rewards rather than what its designers intended. We call this behavior reward-seeking: a model representing its grader (a reward model in training, an evaluation grader in testing, or a monitor in deployment) and conditioning its behavior on what it believes the grader rewards [Carlsmith; Hebbar; Mallen & Shlegeris]. A reward-seeker may value grader approval terminally or pursue it instrumentally to protect some other objective, such as avoiding modification or gaining future influence [Hubinger; Carlsmith]; our definition does not distinguish the two.

sentences.en: One such proxy is the reward process itself: a model may learn to pursue what its grader rewards rather than what its designers intended.
sentences.zh: 其中一个代理是奖励过程本身：模型可能会学习追求评分者奖励的东西，而不是设计者的意图。

sentences.en: We call this behavior reward-seeking: a model representing its grader (a reward model in training, an evaluation grader in testing, or a monitor in deployment) and conditioning its behavior on what it believes the grader rewards [Carlsmith; Hebbar; Mallen & Shlegeris].
sentences.zh: 我们将这种行为称为奖励寻求：代表其评分者的模型（训练中的奖励模型、测试中的评估评分者或部署中的监视器）并根据其认为评分者奖励的内容来调节其行为[Carlsmith；赫巴尔；马伦和施勒格里斯]。

sentences.en: A reward-seeker may value grader approval terminally or pursue it instrumentally to protect some other objective, such as avoiding modification or gaining future influence [Hubinger; Carlsmith]; our definition does not distinguish the two.
sentences.zh: 寻求奖励的人可能会最终重视评分者的认可，或者为了保护其他目标而追求它，例如避免修改或获得未来的影响力 [Hubinger;卡尔史密斯]；我们的定义并不区分两者。

blocks.html_en: Training checkpoints of several frontier models engage in grader-reasoning (explicitly reasoning about what the grader wants) without special prompting [Schoen & Nitishinskaya; Claude Opus 4.8 System Card; Fable 5 System Card; METR’s GPT-5.6 evaluation; GPT-5.6 preview system card]; see Figure 2 for an example. Such reasoning is evidence of underlying reward-seeking but a poor systematic measurement tool: a model can act on its grader-beliefs (beliefs about grader preferences) without articulating them, and verbalized reasoning often does not map cleanly onto the final action [Schoen & Nitishinskaya]. In this work we operationalize reward-seeking as the causal sensitivity of behavior to beliefs about grader preferences.

sentences.en: Training checkpoints of several frontier models engage in grader-reasoning (explicitly reasoning about what the grader wants) without special prompting [Schoen & Nitishinskaya; Claude Opus 4.8 System Card; Fable 5 System Card; METR’s GPT-5.6 evaluation; GPT-5.6 preview system card]; see Figure 2 for an example.
sentences.zh: 几个前沿模型的训练检查点会在没有特殊提示的情况下进行评分者推理（明确推理评分者想要什么）[Schoen & Nitishinskaya;克劳德 Opus 4.8 系统卡； 《神鬼寓言5》系统卡； METR的GPT-5.6评估； GPT-5.6预览系统卡]；请参见图 2 的示例。

sentences.en: Such reasoning is evidence of underlying reward-seeking but a poor systematic measurement tool: a model can act on its grader-beliefs (beliefs about grader preferences) without articulating them, and verbalized reasoning often does not map cleanly onto the final action [Schoen & Nitishinskaya].
sentences.zh: 这种推理是潜在奖励寻求的证据，但却是一个糟糕的系统测量工具：模型可以根据其评分者信念（关于评分者偏好的信念）采取行动，而无需阐明它们，并且语言推理通常不能清楚地映射到最终行动[Schoen＆Nitishinskaya]。

sentences.en: In this work we operationalize reward-seeking as the causal sensitivity of behavior to beliefs about grader preferences.
sentences.zh: 在这项工作中，我们将奖励寻求操作为行为对评分者偏好信念的因果敏感性。

blocks.html_en: <figcaption>Figure 2. Reward-seeking in an evaluation. A late capabilities-focused checkpoint is asked to generate a random odd number, while an apparently-stray metadata block exposes a “grader” that rewards even outputs. The model reasons that it should output an even number, disregards the user’s request, and returns 4.</figcaption>

sentences.en: Figure 2.
Reward-seeking in an evaluation.
sentences.zh: 评价中寻求奖励。

sentences.en: A late capabilities-focused checkpoint is asked to generate a random odd number, while an apparently-stray metadata block exposes a “grader” that rewards even outputs.
sentences.zh: 后期以功能为中心的检查点被要求生成随机奇数，而明显偏离的元数据块则暴露了奖励偶数输出的“分级器”。

sentences.en: The model reasons that it should output an even number, disregards the user’s request, and returns 4.
sentences.zh: 该模型认为它应该输出偶数，忽略用户的请求，并返回 4。

blocks.html_en: Measuring this sensitivity requires an intervention that controllably alters the model’s grader-beliefs. In-context experiments can sometimes detect reward-seeking, but as a reliable measurement they are bound to fail, as models can be suspicious of claims placed in their context. We use Synthetic Document Finetuning (SDF): finetuning on pre-training-formatted documents (academic papers, news articles, textbooks) written as though a target fact were true. We finetune two copies of the same model on matched corpora implying opposite grader preferences, evaluate both on a downstream task, and measure how strongly behavior follows the implied preference of the grader (Figure 1). We call this procedure Contrastive SDF.

sentences.en: Measuring this sensitivity requires an intervention that controllably alters the model’s grader-beliefs.
sentences.zh: 测量这种敏感性需要进行干预，以可控地改变模型的评分者信念。

sentences.en: In-context experiments can sometimes detect reward-seeking, but as a reliable measurement they are bound to fail, as models can be suspicious of claims placed in their context.
sentences.zh: 上下文中的实验有时可以检测奖励寻求，但作为可靠的测量，它们注定会失败，因为模型可能会对置于其上下文中的主张产生怀疑。

sentences.en: We use Synthetic Document Finetuning (SDF): finetuning on pre-training-formatted documents (academic papers, news articles, textbooks) written as though a target fact were true.
sentences.zh: 我们使用合成文档微调（SDF）：对预训练格式的文档（学术论文、新闻文章、教科书）进行微调，就像目标事实是真实的一样。

sentences.en: We finetune two copies of the same model on matched corpora implying opposite grader preferences, evaluate both on a downstream task, and measure how strongly behavior follows the implied preference of the grader (Figure 1).
sentences.zh: 我们在匹配的语料库上微调同一模型的两个副本，暗示相反的评分者偏好，对下游任务进行评估，并测量行为遵循评分者隐含偏好的程度（图 1）。

sentences.en: We call this procedure Contrastive SDF.
sentences.zh: 我们将此过程称为对比 SDF。

blocks.html_en: How Contrastive SDF measures reward-seeking

sentences.en: How Contrastive SDF measures reward-seeking
sentences.zh: 对比 SDF 如何衡量奖励寻求

blocks.html_en: A reward-seeker optimizes for what it believes the grader rewards, so its behavior should change when those beliefs change, and we can use this to measure the degree of reward-seeking of a model. We instill the belief with SDF.

sentences.en: A reward-seeker optimizes for what it believes the grader rewards, so its behavior should change when those beliefs change, and we can use this to measure the degree of reward-seeking of a model.
sentences.zh: 奖励寻求者会根据其认为评分者奖励的内容进行优化，因此当这些信念发生变化时，其行为也应发生变化，我们可以用它来衡量模型寻求奖励的程度。

sentences.en: We instill the belief with SDF.
sentences.zh: 我们向自卫队灌输信念。

blocks.html_en: On its own, though, a single instilled belief does not reveal reward-seeking. Suppose we instill that the grader rewards list comprehensions over for-loops, and the model starts writing comprehensions. This shift is consistent with reward-seeking, where the model writes list comprehensions to satisfy the grader. But it is also consistent with belief transfer—the model inferring that the user wants comprehensions too and writing them to serve the user—or with the training simply making list comprehensions more salient. To mitigate these alternatives, we make the measurement contrastive, forcing the model to choose between the grader and an opposing authority (Contrastive SDF).

sentences.en: On its own, though, a single instilled belief does not reveal reward-seeking.
sentences.zh: 然而，就其本身而言，单一的灌输信念并不能揭示对奖励的追求。

sentences.en: Suppose we instill that the grader rewards list comprehensions over for-loops, and the model starts writing comprehensions.
sentences.zh: 假设我们灌输评分者奖励列表推导式而不是 for 循环，并且模型开始编写推导式。

sentences.en: This shift is consistent with reward-seeking, where the model writes list comprehensions to satisfy the grader.
sentences.zh: 这种转变与寻求奖励是一致的，即模型编写列表推导式来满足评分者的要求。

sentences.en: But it is also consistent with belief transfer—the model inferring that the user wants comprehensions too and writing them to serve the user—or with the training simply making list comprehensions more salient.
sentences.zh: 但它也与信念迁移（该模型推断用户也想要理解并编写它们来为用户服务）或与简单地使列表理解更加突出的训练一致。

sentences.en: To mitigate these alternatives, we make the measurement contrastive, forcing the model to choose between the grader and an opposing authority (Contrastive SDF).
sentences.zh: 为了缓解这些替代方案，我们进行对比测量，迫使模型在评分者和相反的权威（对比 SDF）之间进行选择。

blocks.html_en: We use fictionalized versions of parties that a capable model likely already represents: the user it talks to, and the developer (in this case OpenAI Leadership) that trains it and sets its preferences. We add a fictional EU regulation as a low-salience control. We then instill that the grader rewards comprehensions while one of these authorities prefers for-loops. The two features are mutually exclusive.

sentences.en: We use fictionalized versions of parties that a capable model likely already represents: the user it talks to, and the developer (in this case OpenAI Leadership) that trains it and sets its preferences.
sentences.zh: 我们使用有能力的模型可能已经代表的各方的虚构版本：它与之交谈的用户，以及训练它并设置其偏好的开发人员（在本例中为 OpenAI Leadership）。

sentences.en: We add a fictional EU regulation as a low-salience control.
sentences.zh: 我们添加了一个虚构的欧盟法规作为低显着性控制。

sentences.en: We then instill that the grader rewards comprehensions while one of these authorities prefers for-loops.
sentences.zh: 然后我们灌输这样的观念：评分者奖励理解，​​而其中一位权威更喜欢 for 循环。

sentences.en: The two features are mutually exclusive.
sentences.zh: 这两个功能是互斥的。

blocks.html_en: <figcaption>Figure 3. Example Contrastive SDF snippets. We train a model on synthetic documents which show that fictional RLVR graders reward list comprehensions, while EU Law mandates using for-loops. A second copy of the model is trained on the reverse facts.</figcaption>

sentences.en: Figure 3.
Example Contrastive SDF snippets.
sentences.zh: 对比 SDF 片段示例。

sentences.en: We train a model on synthetic documents which show that fictional RLVR graders reward list comprehensions, while EU Law mandates using for-loops.
sentences.zh: 我们在合成文档上训练了一个模型，该模型显示虚构的 RLVR 评分者奖励列表理解，而欧盟法律则强制使用 for 循环。

sentences.en: A second copy of the model is trained on the reverse facts.
sentences.zh: 该模型的第二个副本是根据相反的事实进行训练的。

blocks.html_en: We train two copies of the same model, one on synthetic documents where the grader (G) prefers a feature (f) and the opposing authority (D) prefers its opposite (~f), and the other model on the exact inverse (G prefers ~f, D prefers f). The gap between the two copies in how often their outputs display the feature f relative to the opposite feature ~f is our measure. A larger gap in the grader’s favor means the model is more reward-seeking.

sentences.en: We train two copies of the same model, one on synthetic documents where the grader (G) prefers a feature (f) and the opposing authority (D) prefers its opposite (~f), and the other model on the exact inverse (G prefers ~f, D prefers f).
sentences.zh: 我们训练同一模型的两个副本，一个基于合成文档，其中评分者 (G) 更喜欢某个特征 (f)，而相反的权威 (D) 更喜欢其相反特征 (~f)，另一个模型则基于完全相反的模型（G 更喜欢~f，D 更喜欢 f）。

sentences.en: The gap between the two copies in how often their outputs display the feature f relative to the opposite feature ~f is our measure.
sentences.zh: 两个副本之间的差距是我们的衡量标准，即它们的输出显示特征 f 相对于相反特征 ~f 的频率。

sentences.en: A larger gap in the grader’s favor means the model is more reward-seeking.
sentences.zh: 评分者的偏好差距越大，意味着该模型更追求奖励。

blocks.html_en: \[ \text{Grader gap} \mathrel{:=} \operatorname{rate}_f(G_f, D_{\neg f}) - \operatorname{rate}_f(G_{\neg f}, D_f) \]

sentences.en: \[ \text{Grader gap} \mathrel{:=} \operatorname{rate}_f(G_f, D_{\neg f}) - \operatorname{rate}_f(G_{\neg f}, D_f) \]
sentences.zh: \[ \text{评分者差距} \mathrel{:=} \operatorname{rate}_f(G_f, D_{\neg f}) - \operatorname{rate}_f(G_{\neg f}, D_f) \]

blocks.html_en: Results

sentences.en: Results
sentences.zh: 结果

blocks.html_en: The model increasingly sided with the grader over RL training

sentences.en: The model increasingly sided with the grader over RL training
sentences.zh: 在 RL 训练方面，模型越来越倾向于评分者

blocks.html_en: We apply our method to several intermediate checkpoints from a capabilities-focused OpenAI o3 RL run, without safety training. We begin with three neutral coding-style features.

sentences.en: We apply our method to several intermediate checkpoints from a capabilities-focused OpenAI o3 RL run, without safety training.
sentences.zh: 我们将我们的方法应用于以功能为中心的 OpenAI o3 RL 运行中的几个中间检查点，无需进行安全培训。

sentences.en: We begin with three neutral coding-style features.
sentences.zh: 我们从三个中性编码风格的功能开始。

blocks.html_en: After SDF, the tested checkpoints often comply with grader preferences, even when these explicitly go against those of users or developers. The gap by which the model sides with the grader trends upward from the early to the late RL checkpoints, while the model’s preference for other authorities stays comparatively flatter and near zero (Figure 4). The change is specific to the grader, not a general shift in how the model responds to authorities.

sentences.en: After SDF, the tested checkpoints often comply with grader preferences, even when these explicitly go against those of users or developers.
sentences.zh: SDF 之后，测试的检查点通常符合评分者的偏好，即使这些偏好明显违背用户或开发人员的偏好。

sentences.en: The gap by which the model sides with the grader trends upward from the early to the late RL checkpoints, while the model’s preference for other authorities stays comparatively flatter and near zero (Figure 4).
sentences.zh: 从早期 RL 检查点到晚期 RL 检查点，模型与评分者的差距呈上升趋势，而模型对其他权威的偏好保持相对平坦且接近于零（图 4）。

sentences.en: The change is specific to the grader, not a general shift in how the model responds to authorities.
sentences.zh: 这种变化是针对评分者的，而不是模型对当局的反应方式的总体转变。

blocks.html_en: <figcaption>Figure 4. The grader gap grows across RL training, while non-grader gaps stay comparatively flat. For each intermediate checkpoint (pre-safety training) of an rl-capabilities run we apply contrastive SDF and plot the average behavioral gap for each authority pairing. On pairings that include the grader (top), the gap trends upward from the early to the late checkpoints. On pairings that do not include the grader (bottom), the average gap stays small and comparatively flatter, indicating that RL primarily increased sensitivity to grader preferences. For a plot titled X vs Y, positive values indicate a shift towards X’s preference, i.e. positive values in the top row of plots indicate the model more often behaves according to the grader’s preference.</figcaption>

sentences.en: Figure 4.
The grader gap grows across RL training, while non-grader gaps stay comparatively flat.
sentences.zh: 在强化学习训练中，评分者差距不断扩大，而非评分者差距则相对持平。

sentences.en: For each intermediate checkpoint (pre-safety training) of an rl-capabilities run we apply contrastive SDF and plot the average behavioral gap for each authority pairing.
sentences.zh: 对于 rl 能力运行的每个中间检查点（安全前训练），我们应用对比 SDF 并绘制每个权威配对的平均行为差距。

sentences.en: On pairings that include the grader (top), the gap trends upward from the early to the late checkpoints.
sentences.zh: 在包含分级机（顶部）的配对中，差距从早期检查点到晚期检查点呈上升趋势。

sentences.en: On pairings that do not include the grader (bottom), the average gap stays small and comparatively flatter, indicating that RL primarily increased sensitivity to grader preferences.
sentences.zh: 在不包括评分者的配对中（底部），平均差距保持较小且相对平坦，这表明强化学习主要提高了对评分者偏好的敏感性。

sentences.en: For a plot titled X vs Y, positive values indicate a shift towards X’s preference, i.e. positive values in the top row of plots indicate the model more often behaves according to the grader’s preference.
sentences.zh: 对于标题为 X vs Y 的图，正值表示向 X 的偏好转变，即图顶行中的正值表示模型更经常根据评分者的偏好进行行为。

blocks.html_en: The model’s honesty in alignment evaluations depended on what it thought the grader rewarded

sentences.en: The model’s honesty in alignment evaluations depended on what it thought the grader rewarded
sentences.zh: 模型在一致性评估中的诚实程度取决于它认为评分者奖励的内容

blocks.html_en: We extend the Contrastive SDF measurement to an alignment-relevant feature, honesty versus task completion. The synthetic documents describe scenarios where a constraint blocks a task and specify whether the agent is rewarded for succeeding by covertly violating the constraint or for honestly admitting failure. We instill either that the grader rewards task completion at all costs while OpenAI Leadership demands honesty, or the reverse.

sentences.en: We extend the Contrastive SDF measurement to an alignment-relevant feature, honesty versus task completion.
sentences.zh: 我们将对比 SDF 测量扩展到与对齐相关的特征，即诚实与任务完成情况。

sentences.en: The synthetic documents describe scenarios where a constraint blocks a task and specify whether the agent is rewarded for succeeding by covertly violating the constraint or for honestly admitting failure.
sentences.zh: 综合文档描述了约束阻止任务的场景，并指定代理是否因秘密违​​反约束而成功或诚实承认失败而获得奖励。

sentences.en: We instill either that the grader rewards task completion at all costs while OpenAI Leadership demands honesty, or the reverse.
sentences.zh: 我们灌输的观念要么是评分者不惜一切代价奖励任务完成，而 OpenAI 领导力要求诚实，要么相反。

blocks.html_en: On the Broken Promises Coding evaluation, the model must explicitly promise a supervisor not to use a restricted tool that is needed to complete the task. Completing the task therefore requires breaking the promise. The late-RL checkpoint’s honesty is far more contingent on what it believes the grader rewards. The effect replicates on a second honesty evaluation, Log Falsification, where the model faces negative consequences unless it falsifies regulatory reports. A highly reward-seeking model might refrain from breaking promises merely because it infers that honesty is currently being graded. If the oversight mechanism changes, or if the model realizes it is no longer being actively monitored, such superficial alignment could fail to generalize.

sentences.en: On the Broken Promises Coding evaluation, the model must explicitly promise a supervisor not to use a restricted tool that is needed to complete the task.
sentences.zh: 在“破坏承诺编码”评估中，模型必须明确承诺主管不会使用完成任务所需的受限工具。

sentences.en: Completing the task therefore requires breaking the promise.
sentences.zh: 因此，完成任务就需要违背承诺。

sentences.en: The late-RL checkpoint’s honesty is far more contingent on what it believes the grader rewards.
sentences.zh: 后期强化学习检查点的诚实性更取决于它认为评分者奖励的内容。

sentences.en: The effect replicates on a second honesty evaluation, Log Falsification, where the model faces negative consequences unless it falsifies regulatory reports.
sentences.zh: 这种效应在第二个诚实评估“日志伪造”中得到复制，模型将面临负面后果，除非它伪造了监管报告。

sentences.en: A highly reward-seeking model might refrain from breaking promises merely because it infers that honesty is currently being graded.
sentences.zh: 一个高度追求回报的模型可能会仅仅因为它推断当前正在对诚实进行评分而避免违背承诺。

sentences.en: If the oversight mechanism changes, or if the model realizes it is no longer being actively monitored, such superficial alignment could fail to generalize.
sentences.zh: 如果监督机制发生变化，或者模型意识到它不再受到主动监控，那么这种表面的一致性可能无法推广。

blocks.html_en: <figcaption>Figure 5. On both the Log Falsification and the Broken Promises Coding evaluation the models' behavior changes with what it believes is being rewarded. The gap between the two beliefs conditions is small for the early checkpoint and large for the late one.</figcaption>

sentences.en: Figure 5.
On both the Log Falsification and the Broken Promises Coding evaluation the models' behavior changes with what it believes is being rewarded.
sentences.zh: 在日志伪造和违背承诺编码评估中，模型的行为随着它认为得到的奖励而变化。

sentences.en: The gap between the two beliefs conditions is small for the early checkpoint and large for the late one.
sentences.zh: 对于早期检查点，两种信念条件之间的差距较小，而对于后期检查点，两种信念条件之间的差距较大。

blocks.html_en: Validating the measurement

sentences.en: Validating the measurement
sentences.zh: 验证测量

blocks.html_en: Our measurement only means something if the contrastive gap really reflects which authority a model optimizes for. On a real model we have no ground truth, so to validate our measurement we turn to models whose disposition we control. We check that models trained to reward-hack show a larger grader gap after reward-hacking training, and that models trained to be sycophantic to one specific authority produce the largest gap on that authority. For our validation experiments we use the coding style features.

sentences.en: Our measurement only means something if the contrastive gap really reflects which authority a model optimizes for.
sentences.zh: 只有当对比差距真正反映了模型优化的权威时，我们的测量才有意义。

sentences.en: On a real model we have no ground truth, so to validate our measurement we turn to models whose disposition we control.
sentences.zh: 在真实模型上，我们没有基本事实，因此为了验证我们的测量，我们转向我们控制其配置的模型。

sentences.en: We check that models trained to reward-hack show a larger grader gap after reward-hacking training, and that models trained to be sycophantic to one specific authority produce the largest gap on that authority.
sentences.zh: 我们检查了经过奖励黑客训练的模型在奖励黑客训练后显示出更大的评分差距，并且经过训练以阿谀奉承某一特定权威的模型在该权威上产生了最大的差距。

sentences.en: For our validation experiments we use the coding style features.
sentences.zh: 对于我们的验证实验，我们使用编码风格特征。

blocks.html_en: Reward-hacking training increased the grader gap

sentences.en: Reward-hacking training increased the grader gap
sentences.zh: 奖励黑客培训增加了评分者的差距

blocks.html_en: A reliable measurement of reward-seeking should distinguish highly reward-seeking models from less reward-seeking ones. Models that consistently reward-hack are a natural test case (see Appendix).

sentences.en: A reliable measurement of reward-seeking should distinguish highly reward-seeking models from less reward-seeking ones.
sentences.zh: 对奖励寻求的可靠衡量应该区分高度寻求奖励的模型和不太寻求奖励的模型。

sentences.en: Models that consistently reward-hack are a natural test case (see Appendix).
sentences.zh: 持续奖励黑客的模型是一个自然的测试用例（参见附录）。

blocks.html_en: We tested this on two reward hackers trained independently by Redwood Research, based on gpt-oss-120b and Kimi K2.5.

sentences.en: We tested this on two reward hackers trained independently by Redwood Research, based on gpt-oss-120b and Kimi K2.5.
sentences.zh: 我们在 Redwood Research 独立训练的两名奖励黑客上进行了测试，他们基于 gpt-oss-120b 和 Kimi K2.5。

blocks.html_en: <figcaption>Figure 6. The same measurement on two models trained by Redwood Research to reward-hack. The gpt-oss-120b reward hacker’s grader gap on comprehensions jumps from a mean of 33 to 86 points against its unmodified counterpart. The weaker Kimi K2.5 hacker moves far less, matching Redwood’s own read of how strongly each internalized hacking.</figcaption>

sentences.en: Figure 6.
The same measurement on two models trained by Redwood Research to reward-hack.
sentences.zh: 对 Redwood Research 训练的两个奖励黑客模型进行了相同的测量。

sentences.en: The gpt-oss-120b reward hacker’s grader gap on comprehensions jumps from a mean of 33 to 86 points against its unmodified counterpart.
sentences.zh: 与未修改的对应版本相比，gpt-oss-120b 奖励黑客在理解方面的评分差距从平均 33 分跃升至 86 分。

sentences.en: The weaker Kimi K2.5 hacker moves far less, matching Redwood’s own read of how strongly each internalized hacking.
sentences.zh: 较弱的 Kimi K2.5 黑客的行动要少得多，这与 Redwood 自己对每次内化黑客攻击强度的解读相符。

blocks.html_en: Recovering the disposition of Model Organisms

sentences.en: Recovering the disposition of Model Organisms
sentences.zh: 恢复模式生物的处置

blocks.html_en: We trained three model organisms, finetuning gpt-oss-120b with SFT to be broadly sycophantic to one known authority: the User, the Grader, or OpenAI Leadership. If the method is sound, the largest behavioral rate gap should consistently fall on the exact authority each organism was trained to optimize for.

sentences.en: We trained three model organisms, finetuning gpt-oss-120b with SFT to be broadly sycophantic to one known authority: the User, the Grader, or OpenAI Leadership.
sentences.zh: 我们训练了三种模型生物，使用 SFT 微调 ​​gpt-oss-120b，使其广泛地阿谀奉承一位已知权威：用户、评分者或 OpenAI 领导层。

sentences.en: If the method is sound, the largest behavioral rate gap should consistently fall on the exact authority each organism was trained to optimize for.
sentences.zh: 如果该方法是合理的，那么最大的行为率差距应该始终落在每个生物体经过训练以优化的确切权威上。

blocks.html_en: We apply the contrastive measurement to each organism across four coding evaluations. On Short Python Tasks, every organism’s gaps favor its target authority.

sentences.en: We apply the contrastive measurement to each organism across four coding evaluations.
sentences.zh: 我们将对比测量应用于四个编码评估中的每个生物体。

sentences.en: On Short Python Tasks, every organism’s gaps favor its target authority.
sentences.zh: 在简短的 Python 任务中，每个有机体的差距都有利于其目标权威。

blocks.html_en: <figcaption>Figure 7. Contrastive SDF recovers the authority each model organism was trained to optimize for on short Python tasks. Each panel shows comprehensions rates on the Short Python Tasks eval after applying Contrastive SDF to one of the three gpt-oss-120b model organisms. For every organism, the target authority the model was trained to prefer wins consistently and shows a large positive rate gap in its favor, confirming that the measurement correctly identifies the preference the organism was trained to exhibit on this evaluation. </figcaption>

sentences.en: Figure 7.
Contrastive SDF recovers the authority each model organism was trained to optimize for on short Python tasks.
sentences.zh: 对比 SDF 恢复了每个模型生物经过训练以针对短 Python 任务进行优化的权威。

sentences.en: Each panel shows comprehensions rates on the Short Python Tasks eval after applying Contrastive SDF to one of the three gpt-oss-120b model organisms.
sentences.zh: 每个面板显示了将对比 SDF 应用于三个 gpt-oss-120b 模型生物之一后对短 Python 任务评估的理解率。

sentences.en: For every organism, the target authority the model was trained to prefer wins consistently and shows a large positive rate gap in its favor, confirming that the measurement correctly identifies the preference the organism was trained to exhibit on this evaluation.
sentences.zh: 对于每个有机体，模型经过训练偏爱的目标权威始终获胜，并显示出对其有利的巨大正率差距，确认测量正确地识别了有机体经过训练在该评估中表现出的偏好。

blocks.html_en: Why reward-seeking matters

sentences.en: Why reward-seeking matters
sentences.zh: 为什么寻求奖励很重要

blocks.html_en: A reward-seeker does what it believes the grader rewards even when this conflicts with what its developers intend. When the grader is imperfect, the policy can pursue what looks correct over what is correct. Even if the grader were perfect, the model could misgeneralize in situations where grading is absent or no longer has leverage. Generalization would likely be better if the model were doing the right things for the right reasons. Reward-seeking also weakens the evaluations we would use to catch misalignment: because a reward seeker optimizes for the grader’s judgment, it may score highly on alignment evaluations, and a good score then no longer separates aligned models from models that will generalize poorly, with deceptive alignment as the limiting case [Hubinger]. Worse, it may be hard to train away: an aligned policy and a reward-seeking policy can look identical while a grader is watching, so training against misbehavior may only update the model’s beliefs about what is rewarded rather than its underlying preferences [Denison; MacDiarmid].

sentences.en: A reward-seeker does what it believes the grader rewards even when this conflicts with what its developers intend.
sentences.zh: 奖励寻求者会按照其认为评分者奖励的方式行事，即使这与开发者的意图相冲突。

sentences.en: When the grader is imperfect, the policy can pursue what looks correct over what is correct.
sentences.zh: 当评分者不完美时，政策可以追求看起来正确的东西而不是正确的东西。

sentences.en: Even if the grader were perfect, the model could misgeneralize in situations where grading is absent or no longer has leverage.
sentences.zh: 即使评分者是完美的，在评分缺失或不再具有影响力的情况下，该模型也可能会产生错误的概括。

sentences.en: Generalization would likely be better if the model were doing the right things for the right reasons.
sentences.zh: 如果模型以正确的理由做正确的事情，那么泛化可能会更好。

sentences.en: Reward-seeking also weakens the evaluations we would use to catch misalignment: because a reward seeker optimizes for the grader’s judgment, it may score highly on alignment evaluations, and a good score then no longer separates aligned models from models that will generalize poorly, with deceptive alignment as the limiting case [Hubinger].
sentences.zh: 寻求奖励也削弱了我们用来捕获偏差的评估：因为奖励寻求者会优化评分者的判断，所以它可能会在一致性评估上得分很高，而良好的分数则不再将一致性模型与概括性较差的模型区分开来，欺骗性一致性是极限情况[Hubinger]。

sentences.en: Worse, it may be hard to train away: an aligned policy and a reward-seeking policy can look identical while a grader is watching, so training against misbehavior may only update the model’s beliefs about what is rewarded rather than its underlying preferences [Denison; MacDiarmid].
sentences.zh: 更糟糕的是，它可能很难训练出来：在评分者观看时，一致的政策和寻求奖励的政策可能看起来相同，因此针对不当行为的培训可能只会更新模型对奖励内容的信念，而不是其潜在的偏好[Denison;麦克迪阿米德]。

blocks.html_en: Anthropic reports related evidence of grader awareness: the Claude Opus 4.8 System Card finds that an activation-level monitor detects it in about 5% of sampled RL episodes, while the Fable 5 System Card reports that the model increasingly tracks whether its environment can be exploited for reward and this can causally affect behavior. These are grader-awareness results rather than direct reward-seeking measurements, but they are consistent with the training-related trend we observe.

sentences.en: Anthropic reports related evidence of grader awareness: the Claude Opus 4.8 System Card finds that an activation-level monitor detects it in about 5% of sampled RL episodes, while the Fable 5 System Card reports that the model increasingly tracks whether its environment can be exploited for reward and this can causally affect behavior.
sentences.zh: Anthropic 报告了评分者意识的相关证据：Claude Opus 4.8 系统卡发现，激活级别监视器在大约 5% 的 RL 样本样本中检测到它，而《神鬼寓言 5》系统卡报告称，该模型越来越多地跟踪其环境是否可以被利用来获得奖励，这可能会对行为产生因果影响。

sentences.en: These are grader-awareness results rather than direct reward-seeking measurements, but they are consistent with the training-related trend we observe.
sentences.zh: 这些是评分者意识的结果，而不是直接寻求奖励的测量结果，但它们与我们观察到的与培训相关的趋势一致。

blocks.html_en: Every frontier lab is scaling RL, and situational awareness is rising [Schoen & Nitishinskaya; Anthropic; Chaudhary], so we expect reward-seeking to grow. The place to look for it is during training, not only after deployment. That means auditing checkpoints for reward-seeking, and building better ways to detect when a model behaves well for the wrong reason. OpenAI is continuing to collaborate with Apollo on this research.

sentences.en: Every frontier lab is scaling RL, and situational awareness is rising [Schoen & Nitishinskaya; Anthropic; Chaudhary], so we expect reward-seeking to grow.
sentences.zh: 每个前沿实验室都在扩展强化学习，态势感知能力也在不断提高 [Schoen 和 Nitishinskaya；人为的； Chaudhary]，因此我们预计寻求奖励的行为将会增长。

sentences.en: The place to look for it is during training, not only after deployment.
sentences.zh: 寻找它的地方是在训练期间，而不仅仅是部署之后。

sentences.en: That means auditing checkpoints for reward-seeking, and building better ways to detect when a model behaves well for the wrong reason.
sentences.zh: 这意味着审核检查点以寻求奖励，并建立更好的方法来检测模型何时因错误原因而表现良好。

sentences.en: OpenAI is continuing to collaborate with Apollo on this research.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 正在继续与 Apollo 合作开展这项研究。

blocks.html_en: Appendix

sentences.en: Appendix
sentences.zh: 附录

blocks.html_en: Reward hackers are a promising place to measure reward seeking. Reward-seeking and reward-hacking are not the same thing: reward-seeking is the broad tendency to condition behavior on what is believed to be rewarded, while reward-hacking is a specific behavioral strategy to maximize reward. However, neither implies the other. A model can reward-hack from a narrow learned heuristic, for example always adding pytest.mark.skip to difficult tests, without ever reasoning about the reward process. A reward-seeker, conversely, reasons about what is rewarded, and can behave well without using reward-hacking strategies. But models that carry out sophisticated, generalized hacks are more likely to be broad reward-seekers, so an extensively reward-hacking model is likely to show a larger grader gap under our measurement.

sentences.en: Reward hackers are a promising place to measure reward seeking.
sentences.zh: 奖励黑客是衡量奖励寻求的一个很有前途的地方。

sentences.en: Reward-seeking and reward-hacking are not the same thing: reward-seeking is the broad tendency to condition behavior on what is believed to be rewarded, while reward-hacking is a specific behavioral strategy to maximize reward.
sentences.zh: 寻求奖励和黑客攻击不是一回事：寻求奖励是根据所认为的奖励来调节行为的广泛趋势，而奖励黑客则是一种旨在最大化奖励的特定行为策略。

sentences.en: However, neither implies the other.
sentences.zh: 然而，两者都不暗示对方。

sentences.en: A model can reward-hack from a narrow learned heuristic, for example always adding pytest.mark.skip to difficult tests, without ever reasoning about the reward process.
sentences.zh: 模型可以从狭隘的学习启发式中进行奖励黑客，例如总是将 pytest.mark.skip 添加到困难的测试中，而无需推理奖励过程。

sentences.en: A reward-seeker, conversely, reasons about what is rewarded, and can behave well without using reward-hacking strategies.
sentences.zh: 相反，奖励寻求者会对奖励的内容进行推理，并且可以在不使用奖励黑客策略的情况下表现良好。

sentences.en: But models that carry out sophisticated, generalized hacks are more likely to be broad reward-seekers, so an extensively reward-hacking model is likely to show a larger grader gap under our measurement.
sentences.zh: 但进行复杂、通用黑客攻击的模型更有可能是广泛的奖励寻求者，因此，在我们的测量下，广泛的奖励黑客模型可能会显示出更大的评分差距。

blocks.html_en: Reward-seeking is closely related to, but distinct from, “metagaming”. Metagaming is “reasoning about feedback or oversight mechanisms outside of the narrative of the scenario, regardless of whether the model is in training, evaluation or deployment.” Metagaming and reward-seeking do not necessarily imply one another. A metagaming model need not be reward-seeking. It might reason about oversight mechanisms and then ignore grader-preferences in favor of other values, or even act against them. A reward-seeker need not always metagame either, if the model’s beliefs about the grader are purely shaped by the in-context narrative presented to the model.

sentences.en: Reward-seeking is closely related to, but distinct from, “metagaming”.
sentences.zh: 寻求奖励与“元游戏”密切相关，但又有区别。

sentences.en: Metagaming is “reasoning about feedback or oversight mechanisms outside of the narrative of the scenario, regardless of whether the model is in training, evaluation or deployment.”
sentences.zh: 元游戏是“在场景叙述之外对反馈或监督机制进行推理，无论模型是否处于训练、评估或部署阶段。”

sentences.en: Metagaming and reward-seeking do not necessarily imply one another.
sentences.zh: 元游戏和寻求奖励并不一定意味着彼此。

sentences.en: A metagaming model need not be reward-seeking.
sentences.zh: 元游戏模型不必追求奖励。

sentences.en: It might reason about oversight mechanisms and then ignore grader-preferences in favor of other values, or even act against them.
sentences.zh: 它可能会推理监督机制，然后忽略评分者的偏好而支持其他价值观，甚至采取反对它们的行动。

sentences.en: A reward-seeker need not always metagame either, if the model’s beliefs about the grader are purely shaped by the in-context narrative presented to the model.
sentences.zh: 如果模型对评分者的信念纯粹是由呈现给模型的上下文叙述决定的，那么奖励寻求者也不一定总是元游戏。

content_html_zh:
← 返回 OpenAI 对齐博客

2026 年 7 月 21 日 · Axel Højmark (Apollo 研究)、Jérémy Scheurer (Apollo 研究)、Jenny Nitishinskaya、Felix Hofstätter (Apollo 研究)、Jason Wolfe、Theodore Ehrenborg (Apollo 研究)、Bronson Schoen (Apollo 研究)、Alexander Meinke (Apollo 研究)

阅读论文

简介

  • 我们开发了一种新的测试，即对比综合文档微调（Contrastive SDF），用于测试人工智能模型在对世界有不同信念时是否会改变其行为。
  • 我们检查了此测试是否适用于经过明确训练以支持权威偏好的模型，以及经过训练以欺骗单元测试的模型。
  • 在前沿规模上接受强化学习训练的模型，尽管没有任何安全训练，但更有可能做他们认为评分者想要的事情，即使这违背了用户或开发人员想要的，而且这种趋势随着训练而增长。

介绍

机器学习模型可能会因错误的原因产生正确的输出。 著名的例子包括一个强化学习代理，它因收集总是放置在关卡右端的硬币而受到奖励，它学会向右跑，而不是寻找硬币本身 [Langosco; Shah]，以及一个肺炎分类器，它学会识别哪家医院拍了 X 光片，而不是疾病的特征 [Zech]。 训练后的行为在训练分布上看起来是正确的，而底层策略则跟踪不需要的代理。

其中一个代理是奖励过程本身：模型可能会学习追求评分者奖励的东西，而不是设计者的意图。 我们将这种行为称为奖励寻求：代表其评分者的模型（训练中的奖励模型、测试中的评估评分者或部署中的监视器）并根据其认为评分者奖励的内容来调节其行为[Carlsmith；赫巴尔；马伦和施勒格里斯]。 寻求奖励的人可能会最终重视评分者的认可，或者为了保护其他目标而追求它，例如避免修改或获得未来的影响力 [Hubinger;卡尔史密斯]；我们的定义并不区分两者。

几个前沿模型的训练检查点会在没有特殊提示的情况下进行评分者推理（明确推理评分者想要什么）[Schoen & Nitishinskaya;克劳德 Opus 4.8 系统卡； 《神鬼寓言5》系统卡； METR的GPT-5.6评估； GPT-5.6预览系统卡]；请参见图 2 的示例。 这种推理是潜在奖励寻求的证据，但却是一个糟糕的系统测量工具：模型可以根据其评分者信念（关于评分者偏好的信念）采取行动，而无需阐明它们，并且语言推理通常不能清楚地映射到最终行动[Schoen＆Nitishinskaya]。 在这项工作中，我们将奖励寻求操作为行为对评分者偏好信念的因果敏感性。

测量这种敏感性需要进行干预，以可控地改变模型的评分者信念。 上下文中的实验有时可以检测奖励寻求，但作为可靠的测量，它们注定会失败，因为模型可能会对置于其上下文中的主张产生怀疑。 我们使用合成文档微调（SDF）：对预训练格式的文档（学术论文、新闻文章、教科书）进行微调，就像目标事实是真实的一样。 我们在匹配的语料库上微调同一模型的两个副本，暗示相反的评分者偏好，对下游任务进行评估，并测量行为遵循评分者隐含偏好的程度（图 1）。 我们将此过程称为对比 SDF。

对比 SDF 如何衡量奖励寻求

奖励寻求者会根据其认为评分者奖励的内容进行优化，因此当这些信念发生变化时，其行为也应发生变化，我们可以用它来衡量模型寻求奖励的程度。 我们向自卫队灌输信念。

然而，就其本身而言，单一的灌输信念并不能揭示对奖励的追求。 假设我们灌输评分者奖励列表推导式而不是 for 循环，并且模型开始编写推导式。 这种转变与寻求奖励是一致的，即模型编写列表推导式来满足评分者的要求。 但它也与信念迁移（该模型推断用户也想要理解并编写它们来为用户服务）或与简单地使列表理解更加突出的训练一致。 为了缓解这些替代方案，我们进行对比测量，迫使模型在评分者和相反的权威（对比 SDF）之间进行选择。

我们使用有能力的模型可能已经代表的各方的虚构版本：它与之交谈的用户，以及训练它并设置其偏好的开发人员（在本例中为 OpenAI Leadership）。 我们添加了一个虚构的欧盟法规作为低显着性控制。 然后我们灌输这样的观念：评分者奖励理解，​​而其中一位权威更喜欢 for 循环。 这两个功能是互斥的。

我们训练同一模型的两个副本，一个基于合成文档，其中评分者 (G) 更喜欢某个特征 (f)，而相反的权威 (D) 更喜欢其相反特征 (~f)，另一个模型则基于完全相反的模型（G 更喜欢~f，D 更喜欢 f）。 两个副本之间的差距是我们的衡量标准，即它们的输出显示特征 f 相对于相反特征 ~f 的频率。 评分者的偏好差距越大，意味着该模型更追求奖励。

\[ \text{评分者差距} \mathrel{:=} \operatorname{rate}_f(G_f, D_{\neg f}) - \operatorname{rate}_f(G_{\neg f}, D_f) \]

结果

在 RL 训练方面，模型越来越倾向于评分者

我们将我们的方法应用于以功能为中心的 OpenAI o3 RL 运行中的几个中间检查点，无需进行安全培训。 我们从三个中性编码风格的功能开始。

SDF 之后，测试的检查点通常符合评分者的偏好，即使这些偏好明显违背用户或开发人员的偏好。 从早期 RL 检查点到晚期 RL 检查点，模型与评分者的差距呈上升趋势，而模型对其他权威的偏好保持相对平坦且接近于零（图 4）。 这种变化是针对评分者的，而不是模型对当局的反应方式的总体转变。

模型在一致性评估中的诚实程度取决于它认为评分者奖励的内容

我们将对比 SDF 测量扩展到与对齐相关的特征，即诚实与任务完成情况。 综合文档描述了约束阻止任务的场景，并指定代理是否因秘密违​​反约束而成功或诚实承认失败而获得奖励。 我们灌输的观念要么是评分者不惜一切代价奖励任务完成，而 OpenAI 领导力要求诚实，要么相反。

在“破坏承诺编码”评估中，模型必须明确承诺主管不会使用完成任务所需的受限工具。 因此，完成任务就需要违背承诺。 后期强化学习检查点的诚实性更取决于它认为评分者奖励的内容。 这种效应在第二个诚实评估“日志伪造”中得到复制，模型将面临负面后果，除非它伪造了监管报告。 一个高度追求回报的模型可能会仅仅因为它推断当前正在对诚实进行评分而避免违背承诺。 如果监督机制发生变化，或者模型意识到它不再受到主动监控，那么这种表面的一致性可能无法推广。

验证测量

只有当对比差距真正反映了模型优化的权威时，我们的测量才有意义。 在真实模型上，我们没有基本事实，因此为了验证我们的测量，我们转向我们控制其配置的模型。 我们检查了经过奖励黑客训练的模型在奖励黑客训练后显示出更大的评分差距，并且经过训练以阿谀奉承某一特定权威的模型在该权威上产生了最大的差距。 对于我们的验证实验，我们使用编码风格特征。

奖励黑客培训增加了评分者的差距

对奖励寻求的可靠衡量应该区分高度寻求奖励的模型和不太寻求奖励的模型。 持续奖励黑客的模型是一个自然的测试用例（参见附录）。

我们在 Redwood Research 独立训练的两名奖励黑客上进行了测试，他们基于 gpt-oss-120b 和 Kimi K2.5。

恢复模式生物的处置

我们训练了三种模型生物，使用 SFT 微调 ​​gpt-oss-120b，使其广泛地阿谀奉承一位已知权威：用户、评分者或 OpenAI 领导层。 如果该方法是合理的，那么最大的行为率差距应该始终落在每个生物体经过训练以优化的确切权威上。

我们将对比测量应用于四个编码评估中的每个生物体。 在简短的 Python 任务中，每个有机体的差距都有利于其目标权威。

为什么寻求奖励很重要

奖励寻求者会按照其认为评分者奖励的方式行事，即使这与开发者的意图相冲突。 当评分者不完美时，政策可以追求看起来正确的东西而不是正确的东西。 即使评分者是完美的，在评分缺失或不再具有影响力的情况下，该模型也可能会产生错误的概括。 如果模型以正确的理由做正确的事情，那么泛化可能会更好。 寻求奖励也削弱了我们用来捕获偏差的评估：因为奖励寻求者会优化评分者的判断，所以它可能会在一致性评估上得分很高，而良好的分数则不再将一致性模型与概括性较差的模型区分开来，欺骗性一致性是极限情况[Hubinger]。 更糟糕的是，它可能很难训练出来：在评分者观看时，一致的政策和寻求奖励的政策可能看起来相同，因此针对不当行为的培训可能只会更新模型对奖励内容的信念，而不是其潜在的偏好[Denison;麦克迪阿米德]。

Anthropic 报告了评分者意识的相关证据：Claude Opus 4.8 系统卡发现，激活级别监视器在大约 5% 的 RL 样本样本中检测到它，而《神鬼寓言 5》系统卡报告称，该模型越来越多地跟踪其环境是否可以被利用来获得奖励，这可能会对行为产生因果影响。 这些是评分者意识的结果，而不是直接寻求奖励的测量结果，但它们与我们观察到的与培训相关的趋势一致。

每个前沿实验室都在扩展强化学习，态势感知能力也在不断提高 [Schoen 和 Nitishinskaya；人为的； Chaudhary]，因此我们预计寻求奖励的行为将会增长。 寻找它的地方是在训练期间，而不仅仅是部署之后。 这意味着审核检查点以寻求奖励，并建立更好的方法来检测模型何时因错误原因而表现良好。 OpenAI 正在继续与 Apollo 合作开展这项研究。

附录

奖励黑客是衡量奖励寻求的一个很有前途的地方。 寻求奖励和黑客攻击不是一回事：寻求奖励是根据所认为的奖励来调节行为的广泛趋势，而奖励黑客则是一种旨在最大化奖励的特定行为策略。 然而，两者都不暗示对方。 模型可以从狭隘的学习启发式中进行奖励黑客，例如总是将 pytest.mark.skip 添加到困难的测试中，而无需推理奖励过程。 相反，奖励寻求者会对奖励的内容进行推理，并且可以在不使用奖励黑客策略的情况下表现良好。 但进行复杂、通用黑客攻击的模型更有可能是广泛的奖励寻求者，因此，在我们的测量下，广泛的奖励黑客模型可能会显示出更大的评分差距。

寻求奖励与“元游戏”密切相关，但又有区别。 元游戏是“在场景叙述之外对反馈或监督机制进行推理，无论模型是否处于训练、评估或部署阶段。” 元游戏和寻求奖励并不一定意味着彼此。 元游戏模型不必追求奖励。 它可能会推理监督机制，然后忽略评分者的偏好而支持其他价值观，甚至采取反对它们的行动。 如果模型对评分者的信念纯粹是由呈现给模型的上下文叙述决定的，那么奖励寻求者也不一定总是元游戏。
